title: The knowledge project — Brookfield CEO Connor Teskey on AI Infrastructure, Data Centers, and the Future of Investing
description: Highlights from The knowledge project: Brookfield CEO Connor Teskey on AI Infrastructure, Data Centers, and the Future of Investing

highlights.title: Culture Key Factor
highlights.description: The importance of culture at Brookfield is emphasized as a key factor in its success, with a focus on putting others in a position to succeed more than oneself.

highlights.title: Mentorship Importance
highlights.description: Incredible mentorship is highlighted as a key factor in the guest's success, with a focus on learning from experience and having good judgment.

highlights.title: De-Risking Investments
highlights.description: Brookfield works to de-risk investments by locking in key variables such as construction costs, revenue, and financing, and by taking a long-term approach to investing.

highlights.title: Global Business
highlights.description: Brookfield has a unique business with local boots on the ground teams in various regions, allowing for independence and autonomy in sourcing, executing, and operating investments.

highlights.title: Non-Consensus Ideas
highlights.description: The example of Westinghouse is used to illustrate how Brookfield thinks about taking bets that are maybe non-consensus, with a focus on industrial operating businesses that can be run better using operational expertise.

title: Refactoring Podcast — Leading Beyond the Framework 🧭 — with Richard Hughes-Jones
description: Highlights from Refactoring Podcast: Leading Beyond the Framework 🧭 — with Richard Hughes-Jones

highlights.title: Coaching Journey
highlights.description: The guest shares his experience of writing an article with a former coaching client, providing an honest account of their coaching journey.

highlights.title: Non-Technical Advantage
highlights.description: The guest explains how not having a strong technical background can be an advantage in coaching tech leaders, allowing him to focus on other aspects of their development.

highlights.title: Leadership Challenges
highlights.description: The guest discusses the challenges faced by senior leaders in tech, including ambiguity, complexity, and uncertainty, and how these challenges are still relevant despite the current changes in the tech industry.

highlights.title: Embracing Uncertainty
highlights.description: The guest acknowledges that humans are not wired for uncertainty and that it's hard to articulate and explain how to be comfortable with ambiguity, but recognizes its importance for leaders.

highlights.title: Leadership Development
highlights.description: The guest reflects on the journey of individual contributors to leaders, and how there's a point where conventional frameworks and models may not be enough, requiring a different approach to development.

title: The Diary Of A CEO — Daniel Priestley: Plumbers Will Earn More Than Lawyers! I Predicted 2008, Now I'm Warning About 2029
description: Highlights from The Diary Of A CEO: Daniel Priestley: Plumbers Will Earn More Than Lawyers! I Predicted 2008, Now I'm Warning About 2029

highlights.title: Jevons Paradox Explained
highlights.description: The Jevons Paradox is discussed, highlighting how technological advancements can lead to increased efficiency and create new opportunities and jobs that didn't exist before.

highlights.title: Validating Business Ideas
highlights.description: The importance of validating business ideas is emphasized, and the process of identifying a good idea, validating it, and scaling it is discussed.

highlights.title: Changing Work Preferences
highlights.description: The changing preferences of workers are discussed, with many people wanting to work fewer days, have non-monotonous and creative work, and work with a small group of people they enjoy.

highlights.title: Fundamental Human Needs
highlights.description: The fundamental human need for connection is highlighted, using the example of the Resus Monkeys experiment to illustrate the importance of human connection and community.

highlights.title: Personal Playbooks and AI
highlights.description: The importance of having personal playbooks and using AI to turn them into products and services is discussed, emphasizing the need for content creators to focus on quality over quantity.

title: HBR Ideacast — The Shifting Relationship Between Business and the U.S. Government
description: Highlights from HBR Ideacast: The Shifting Relationship Between Business and the U.S. Government

highlights.title: Pensive C-Suite
highlights.description: The American C-suite is pensive, with strong opinions but a fear of vindictiveness, according to Jeffrey Sonnenfeld.

highlights.title: CEO Dilemma
highlights.description: CEOs face a dilemma in navigating political issues, where they must balance their company's interests with the need to speak out on social issues.

highlights.title: Engaging Trump
highlights.description: Jeffrey Sonnenfeld advises that CEOs can effectively engage with President Trump by presenting a united front and using their collective voice to influence policy.

highlights.title: Social Capital
highlights.description: Alexis de Tocqueville's concept of social capital is highlighted, where business leaders play a crucial role in defining truth and upholding democracy by speaking out on important issues.

highlights.title: CEO Responsibility
highlights.description: CEOs have a responsibility to speak out on social issues that affect their employees, consumers, and shareholders, and to use their trusted voices to endorse the truth and support what is right.
